display.purpose: Share-card message text — the most important words in the product.

label.purpose: Status chips, mood labels, counters, share-card eyebrow.

brand.purpose: The พิมพ์ใจ · Pimjai wordmark only.

shadows.name: card-lift
shadows.purpose: Standard float for popups, drop form, search results.

shadows.name: search-rest
shadows.purpose: Lighter lift for the always-present search bar and brand pill.

shadows.name: warp-glow
shadows.purpose: The single colored glow — warp CTA only.

motion.name: ease-standard
motion.purpose: Default easing for card fade-in, pop, and state transitions.

motion.name: shake
motion.purpose: Wrong-password feedback on the unlock popup.

motion.name: fade-in
motion.purpose: Card and revealed-text entrance; must enhance already-visible content.

components.name: Glass Card
components.description: The universal chrome material — every popup, form, search bar, and pill is built from this floating light-glass.
components.html:
Drop a message ✨

What's on your mind at this exact spot?
components.css: .ds-card-glass { background: rgba(255,255,255,0.82); backdrop-filter: blur(14px) saturate(140%); -webkit-backdrop-filter: blur(14px) saturate(140%); border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.6); border-radius: 24px; padding: 20px; color: #0f172a; box-shadow: 0 10px 30px rgba(0,0,0,0.25); max-width: 360px; } .ds-card-title { font-family: 'Geist Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 700; font-size: 1.125rem; margin: 0 0 8px; } .ds-card-body { font-size: 0.875rem; line-height: 1.45; color: #334155; margin: 0; }

components.name: Submit Button
components.description: The only filled-accent button — the high-commitment 'drop' / 'crack' action.
components.css: .ds-btn-submit { font-family: 'Geist Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 700; font-size: 0.95rem; color: #fff; background: linear-gradient(90deg, #06b6d4, #d946ef, #9333ea); border: none; border-radius: 12px; padding: 12px 16px; width: 100%; box-shadow: 0 8px 20px rgba(217,70,239,0.30); cursor: pointer; transition: transform 0.15s ease, opacity 0.15s ease; } .ds-btn-submit:hover { transform: translateY(-1px); } .ds-btn-submit:active { transform: scale(0.98); } .ds-btn-submit:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #e879f9; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-submit:disabled { opacity: 0.5; cursor: not-allowed; }

components.name: Warp Button
components.description: Signature CTA — teleports to a random stranger's pin. The one permitted neon-glow object.
components.css: .ds-btn-warp { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; font-family: 'Geist Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 700; color: #fff; background: linear-gradient(90deg, #22d3ee, #d946ef, #9333ea); border: none; border-radius: 9999px; padding: 12px 16px; box-shadow: 0 0 25px rgba(157,77,255,0.6); cursor: pointer; transition: transform 0.15s ease; } .ds-btn-warp:active { transform: scale(0.95); } .ds-btn-warp:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #fff; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-warp:disabled { opacity: 0.4; cursor: not-allowed; }

components.name: Glass Input
components.description: Translucent text field on glass; focus shifts the border to Fuchsia Soft, no heavy glow.
components.css: .ds-input { width: 100%; box-sizing: border-box; font-family: 'Geist Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; color: #1e293b; background: rgba(255,255,255,0.70); border: 1px solid #cbd5e1; border-radius: 12px; padding: 12px; outline: none; transition: border-color 0.15s ease; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #94a3b8; } .ds-input:focus { border-color: #e879f9; }

components.name: Search Pill
components.description: Always-present floating search bar — the primary way to travel the globe by place name.
components.css: .ds-search { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; background: rgba(255,255,255,0.82); backdrop-filter: blur(14px) saturate(140%); -webkit-backdrop-filter: blur(14px) saturate(140%); border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.6); border-radius: 9999px; padding: 10px 16px; box-shadow: 0 8px 20px rgba(0,0,0,0.18); max-width: 28rem; } .ds-search-input { flex: 1; border: none; background: transparent; outline: none; font-family: 'Geist Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; color: #1e293b; } .ds-search-input::placeholder { color: #94a3b8; }

components.name: Mood Tile
components.description: Mood selector in the drop form — selected state shows a colored ring. Mood color stays on the message, so the tile itself is neutral until chosen.
components.css: .ds-mood-row { display: flex; gap: 12px; } .ds-mood { flex: 1; display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; gap: 4px; background: rgba(255,255,255,0.6); border: 1px solid #e2e8f0; border-radius: 12px; padding: 8px 0; cursor: pointer; transition: box-shadow 0.15s ease; } .ds-mood-emoji { font-size: 1.5rem; } .ds-mood-label { font-size: 10px; font-weight: 600; color: #64748b; } .ds-mood--on { box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px #f472b6; } .ds-mood:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #e879f9; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Status Chip
components.description: Read-only lock-state indicator. Color is the chip; no background.
components.html: locked
components.css: .ds-status { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 4px; font-family: 'Geist Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 600; } .ds-status--locked { color: #c026d3; } .ds-status--unlocked { color: #059669; }

narrative.northStar: Lit Windows in a Dark City
narrative.overview: Pimjai is a single, full-bleed 3D globe rendered at night, and everything else is a small lit window glowing on its surface. The globe is the dark enormous city; each message a stranger left is a warm rectangle of light you can press your face against. That contrast — vast cold dark against small warm intimate — is the design. The system commits to floating glass over real depth: chrome is built from translucent light-glass cards that lift off the canvas with soft blur and shadow, while the live 3D scene supplies the actual depth. Saturation is rationed hard — night-navy and cool slate neutrals everywhere, and the only fully saturated color belongs to a message's mood. The words carry the hue; the UI never competes for it.
keyCharacteristics: One full-screen globe; UI floats over it and recedes.
Night-navy + cool-slate neutral chrome; mood color is the only saturation.
Light-glass cards (blur + soft shadow) as the universal chrome material.
Soft, tender geometry: generous radii (12–24px), no hard corners.
Expressive effects (warp glow, brand gradient) are rare punctuation, not wallpaper.

rules.name: The Message Owns The Color Rule
rules.body: Saturated mood color belongs to the message and its share card — full stop. Chrome stays night-navy + slate neutral, accented only by Fuchsia Pulse. If a button, panel, or background carries a mood gradient, it is wrong.

rules.name: The Rationed Neon Rule
rules.body: The cyan→fuchsia→amber brand gradient and the warp button's purple glow are the only sanctioned multi-stop neon, each appearing exactly once. Neon gradient anywhere else reads as web3 hype and is forbidden.

rules.name: The One Family Rule
rules.body: Geist Sans does all the work; Geist Mono only for raw coordinates. Never a third typeface or display serif — the restraint lets the message feel personal rather than packaged.

rules.name: The Glass-Or-Nothing Rule
rules.body: Chrome is either floating glass (blur ≥14px, translucency 0.82, soft shadow) or it does not exist. No opaque white panels, no hard-edged solid cards.

dos: Do keep the globe the loudest thing on screen. Chrome recedes; a stranger's words win.
Do reserve all saturated color for the message and its mood — pink #ff2d9d, black #0d0a16, yellow #ffe14d. Chrome stays night-navy + slate.
Do build every piece of chrome as floating glass (blur ≥14px, rgba(255,255,255,0.82), soft shadow).
Do keep accent to Fuchsia Pulse on interactive chrome, used sparingly.
Do reveal pins one at a time, by chance. Serendipity over density.
Do give every animation a prefers-reduced-motion fallback — the warp camera-fly especially must degrade to an instant cut.
Do pair mood with an icon/label, never color alone, for color-blind users.
Do verify glass-card body text and placeholders clear 4.5:1 over bright globe regions; bump slate-400 → slate-500 if a measure fails.
donts: Don't build anything resembling a cluttered social feed — no timelines, counts, likes, follows, badges, leaderboards, trending, profiles, or notification noise.
Don't let the neon brand gradient or warp glow spread beyond their two sanctioned homes (the wordmark; the warp CTA).
Don't put mood color on chrome — no mood-gradient buttons, panels, or backgrounds.
Don't ship opaque flat panels or hard-edged solid cards; it breaks the floating-glass illusion.
Don't introduce a second typeface or a display serif. Geist in many weights only.
Don't use light-gray body text 'for elegance' on the bright glass — it fails contrast and reads as AI default.
Don't gate content visibility behind a class-triggered reveal; the fade-in animations must enhance already-visible content.
